docket_entries.description: NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

ANSWER TO COMPLAINT (ANSWER STRICKEN PER ORDER GRANTING TERMINATING AND MONETARY SANCTIONS AND ISSUING CONTEMPT FILED ON 11/19/15) FILED BY DEFENDANT HOME HEALTH ADVOCATES, INC. DBA HOME CARE ADVOCATES (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS HOME HEALTH ADVOCATES, INC. DBA HOME HEALTH ADVOCATES AND HOME CARE ADVOCATES)

DECLARATION OF JASON P. BROWN IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT HOME HEALTH ADVOCATES, INC. DBA HOME CARE ADVOCATES (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS HOME HEALTH ADVOCATES, INC. DBA HOME HEALTH ADVOCATES AND HOME CARE ADVOCATES)
OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE SUMITTED BY DEFENDANTS IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF DEPIO, ELSIE

LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT HOME HEALTH ADVOCATES, INC. DBA HOME CARE ADVOCATES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ADJUDICATION IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE DECLARATION OF PLAINTIFF ELSIE DEPIO CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING HOW MANY HOURS A DAY PLAINTIFF WORKED FOR DEFENDANT'S CLIENT AND WHAT PLAINTIFF'S TASKS WERE. (ELSIE DEPIO DEC., PARS. 3-9 AND 10-19). THE DECLARATION SHOWS THAT THERE ARE DISPUTED FACTUAL ISSUES REGARDING WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS PAID A MINIMUM WAGE AND WHETHER SHE IS AN EXEMPT PERSONAL ATTENDANT UNDER IWC ORDER 15-2001. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976
MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-31-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication; Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Oct-31-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT HOME HEALTH ADVOCATES, INC. DBA HOME CARE ADVOCATES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication - The motion for summary judgment or adjudication is denied in its entirety. The declaration of Plaintiff Elsie Depio creates a triable issue of material fact regarding how many hours a day Plaintiff worked for defendant's client and what Plaintiff's tasks were. (Elsie Depio dec., pars. 3-9 and 10-19). The declaration shows that there are disputed factual issues regarding whether Plaintiff was paid a minimum wage and whether she is an exempt personal attendant under IWC Order 15-2001.
ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg
